skillName: Find the median from a set of numbers (incl midpoint using a calculator)
misconceptionSummary: Students are thrown off by outliers, thinking the median shifts towards extreme values.

steps.narration: The outlier grabs attention, but it has no power to move the median. Sorting reveals exactly where the middle falls — and the outlier always ends up at the edge.
Once sorted, the outlier is pushed to the very end. It takes up position five out of five — nowhere near the middle.
The middle position is position three. That value is five. The one hundred is nowhere near the middle — it plays no part in this calculation.

visuals.label: Outliers go to the ends — they cannot move the median

steps.narration: Five. Not influenced by one hundred at all. That is one of the most powerful features of the median — extreme values cannot pull it away from the true centre.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: assuming extreme values shift the median. After sorting, outliers sit at the ends and have no effect on the middle position.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: An outlier can only sit at the edge of the sorted list. It cannot reach the middle — and the median only cares about the middle.
